factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: This increase in revenue was driven by [...] price increases.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 29, 2024

factors.summary: Revenue growth is driven by both increased demand and price increases, indicating strong real demand.

evidence.text: This increase in revenue was driven by many factors including [...] price increases and strong customer retention.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 29, 2024

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting favorably, contributing to higher margins and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: The improvement in gross margin [...] was primarily driven by a favorable sales mix and sourcing and productivity initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 29, 2024

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with net cash provided by operating activities increasing.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $1,530.2 million [...] compared to $1,386.7 million for the prior period.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing due to higher share buybacks and dividends.

evidence.text: The increase in cash used in financing activities was due to the increase in share buyback activity and an increase in dividends paid.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are stable, with investments focused on key operating segments.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $294.3 million [...] compared to $307.6 million for the prior period.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is not material, as per management's opinion.

evidence.text: In the opinion of management, the aggregate liability [...] will not have a material adverse effect [...] on the consolidated financial position [...] of Cintas.

factors.summary: Cost actions are delivering measurable savings, particularly in the Uniform Rental and Facility Services segment.

evidence.text: Cost of uniform rental and facility services improved as a percent of revenue, decreasing from 51.2% for the three months ended February 29, 2024, to 50.0% for the three months ended February 28, 2025. This improvement as a percent of revenue was primarily due to more efficient usage of in-service inventory and more efficient routing due to our SmartTruck initiative.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Three Months Ended February 29, 2024

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by initiatives like SmartTruck and sourcing and productivity initiatives.

evidence.text: This improvement as a percent of revenue was primarily due to more efficient usage of in-service inventory and more efficient routing due to our SmartTruck initiative. The improvement in cost of sales as a percent of revenue was primarily due to a favorable sales mix and sourcing and productivity initiatives in the First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Three Months Ended February 29, 2024

factors.summary: The economic environment is a tailwind, with strong revenue growth and improving margins indicating favorable conditions.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 8.4% to $2,609.2 million for the three months ended February 28, 2025, compared to $2,406.2 million for the three months ended February 29, 2024. The organic revenue growth rate, which adjusts for the impact of acquisitions and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, was 7.9%. Revenue growth was positively impacted by 0.9% due to acquisitions and negatively impacted by 0.4% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Three Months Ended February 29, 2024

factors.summary: The company is experiencing strong organic revenue growth, with acceleration noted in both the nine-month and three-month periods. The growth is driven by factors such as increased demand, operational improvements, new business sales, product和服务渗透, price increases, and strong customer retention.

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 13.8%. [...] This increase in revenue was driven by many factors including new business sold by sales representatives, penetration of additional products and services into existing customers, price increases and strong customer retention.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 29, 2024

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth rate for the reportable operating segment was 7.0%. [...] The organic revenue growth rate, which adjusts for the impact of acquisitions and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, was 7.9%
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Three Months Ended February 29, 2024

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, as evidenced by strong customer retention, new business sales, increased product penetration, and revenue growth across both reportable operating segments.

evidence.text: This increase in revenue was driven by many factors including new business sold by sales representatives, penetration of additional products and services into existing customers, price increases and strong customer retention.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 29, 2024

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 8.4% to $2,609.2 million for the three months ended February 28, 2025, compared to $2,406.2 million for the three months ended February 29, 2024. The organic revenue growth rate, which adjusts for the impact of acquisitions and foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, was 7.9%. Revenue growth was positively impacted by 0.9% due to acquisitions and negatively impacted by 0.4% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Three Months Ended February 29, 2024

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins have shown consistent improvement, with both periods indicating expansion driven by favorable sales mix, productivity initiatives, efficient inventory usage, and routing optimizations.

evidence.text: The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 57.3% [...] compared to 55.6% in the prior period. [...] The improvement in gross margin [...] was primarily driven by a favorable sales mix and sourcing and productivity initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 29, 2024

evidence.text: Gross margin as a percent of revenue was 50.0% for the three months ended February 28, 2025, compared to 48.8% for the three months ended February 29, 2024. The improvement in gross margin was primarily the result of more efficient usage of in-service inventory and more efficient routing due to our SmartTruck initiative. Operating income was $609.9 million, or 23.4% of revenue, for the three months ended February 28, 2025, compared to $520.8 million, or 21.6% of revenue, for the three months ended February 29, 2024.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2025 Compared to Three Months Ended February 29, 2024
